card.intuition: A version control system for your database schema, allowing you to gradually modify the structure over time in a safe, reversible way.
card.analogy: Updating a recipe book: instead of throwing away the old book and writing a new one every time you add a spice, you write a small 'sticky note' saying 'Add 1 tsp salt' so everyone knows exactly what changed.
when_to_use: Changing the database structure when multiple developers are working on the same project
Deploying schema changes to production safely
Reverting a broken schema change (Rolling back)
gotchas: Never modify production data directly; always use a migration to keep all environments in sync
Destructive migrations (like dropping a column) should be done in multiple steps: add new column, sync data, then drop old column (Expand-Contract pattern)

steps.annotation: System overview — step through to trace the flow.
Developer writes versioned migration file — up() and down() methods.
Migration tool checks schema_versions table to find pending migrations.
Applies pending migrations in order. Each tracked in version table.
Rollback runs down() in reverse order to undo changes.

exercise.prompt: Complete the migration file logic for adding an 'age' column.
hints: In the 'up' method, you 'ADD' the column
In the 'down' method, you 'DROP' the column to revert
exercise.test_code:
def check(u, d): return u.upper() == "ADD" and d.upper() == "DROP"
assert check("ADD", "DROP")
print("PASS")
